headlines.title: Why Graham Platner’s margin matters in the Maine: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Welcome to From the Politics Desk, a daily newsletter that brings you the NBC News Politics team’s latest reporting and analysis from the White House, Capitol Hill and the campaign trail

headlines.title: Michigan is ‘ready’ if administration sends federal agents to November polls: Lieutenant governor
headlines.description: Michigan Lieutenant Governor Garlin Gilchrist (D) joins Meet the Press NOW to discuss his decision to run for secretary of state. Lt. Gov. Gilchrist also reacts to President Trump’s claims of voter fraud in California.

headlines.title: Kornacki: Will Maine Democrats use the primary to signal concerns about Graham Platner’s scandals?
headlines.description: NBC’s Steve Kornacki and Ryan Nobles preview the primary in Maine as Graham Platner looks to secure the Democratic nomination for the Senate race. NBC’s Kelly O’Donnell and Ben Kamisar discuss the president’s claims of voter fraud in California’s primary.

headlines.title: Trump says Iran shot down U.S. helicopter
headlines.description: President Trump said in a Truth Social post that Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz, with both soldiers rescued by an unmanned boat off the coast of Oman.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Vance refers Walz and Minnesota attorney general to DOJ for fraud investigation
headlines.description: The Justice Department opened a separate investigation this year into the Democratic governor over allegations he helped impede immigration enforcement.

headlines.title: Todd and Julie Chrisley sue their former lawyers for losing case that sent them to jail
headlines.description: Reality stars Todd and Julie Chrisley are suing former defense attorneys for $25 million, blaming them for losing a criminal case that found them guilty of defrauding banks out of millions of dollars

headlines.title: 2020 election denier under scrutiny from fair elections advocates for new role at DOJ
headlines.description: An election denier who tried to overturn Donald Trump’s 2020 election loss is facing scrutiny over his new role in the Justice Department as part of a team investigating the president’s foes.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Graham Platner and big races for governor: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries
headlines.description: Four states vote Tuesday, with Maine’s key Senate race capturing the most national attention and Trump picking sides in South Carolina’s race for governor.

headlines.title: Trump booed at Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of NBA Finals
headlines.description: President Donald Trump attended Madison Square Garden for Game 3 of the NBA Finals on Monday to cheer on his hometown team — but many of his fellow fans didn’t appear thrilled to have him there.

headlines.title: Some Republicans say Trump should close the door on paying out Jan. 6 rioters
headlines.description: Democrats and some Republicans were critical of the president’s defense of his “anti-weaponization” fund on NBC News’ “Meet the Press.” Others believe the idea is dead.
